title: kalmia polifolia subsp. microphylla
intro: Kalmia polifolia subsp. microphylla, also known as the small-leaved Labrador tea, is a subspecies of the Labrador tea plant, which is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating a variety of health issues such as sore throats, coughs, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Kalmia polifolia subsp. microphylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
